rationale: Title X better serves a JACC audience because it states the key comparative result and the clinically important safety finding upfront. The registry size and pediatric population are clear. Title Y is cleaner and names NCDR precisely, yet it mainly lists endpoints and gives less of the study's actual message.
loser_weakness: Title Y is less informative because it withholds the main finding, especially the absence of pacemaker-requiring AV block.
